chapters.title: Letter Sounds
chapters.concept: Every letter has its very own sound! Cat begins with the sound of c. Dog begins with the sound of d. Say the sound, then find the letter. You can do it, superstar!

problems.q: What sound does sun begin with?
problems.why: Sun begins with the s sound, the letter s.

problems.q: What sound does bat begin with?
problems.why: Bat begins with the b sound, the letter b.

problems.q: What sound does dog begin with?
problems.why: Dog begins with the d sound, the letter d.

problems.q: What sound does fish begin with?
problems.why: Fish begins with the f sound, the letter f.

problems.q: What sound does moon begin with?
problems.why: Moon begins with the m sound, the letter m.

problems.q: What sound does apple begin with?
problems.why: Apple begins with the a sound, the letter a.

problems.q: What sound does cake begin with?
problems.why: Cake begins with the c sound, the letter c.

chapters.title: Magic Vowels
chapters.concept: Five letters are super special: a, e, i, o, u. They are the vowels! Every word needs a vowel. Let's find them, magic reader!

problems.why: a is one of the five vowels: a, e, i, o, u.
e is a vowel. The vowels are a, e, i, o, u.
o is a vowel. The vowels are a, e, i, o, u.

problems.q: The vowels are a, e, i, o and ___?
problems.why: The five vowels are a, e, i, o, u.

problems.q: Which vowel is in the middle of pig?
problems.why: p-i-g: the vowel in the middle is i.

problems.q: Which vowel is in the middle of dog?
problems.why: d-o-g: the vowel in the middle is o.

chapters.title: Word Families
chapters.concept: When we blend sounds, we can read words! c - a - t says cat. Words that end the same are a word family, like cat, hat and bat. Let's blend and read!

problems.q: Blend the sounds and read the word.
problems.why: c-a-t blends together to make cat.

problems.q: Blend the sounds and read the word.
problems.why: h-a-t blends together to make hat.

problems.q: Blend the sounds and read the word.
problems.why: d-o-g blends together to make dog.

problems.q: Blend the sounds and read the word.
problems.why: p-i-g blends together to make pig.

problems.q: Blend the sounds and read the word.
problems.why: s-u-n blends together to make sun.

problems.q: Blend the sounds and read the word.
problems.why: b-e-d blends together to make bed.

problems.q: Add b to the -at family. What word do you get?
problems.why: b + at makes bat. It is in the -at family with cat and hat.

chapters.title: Build the Word
chapters.concept: Listen to the word, then tap the letters in the right order to build it! Tap each letter to hear its sound. Ready, spelling star?

chapters.title: Rhyme Time
chapters.concept: Rhyming words sound the same at the end! Cat and hat rhyme. Sun and bun rhyme too. Let's find the words that rhyme!

problems.why: cat and hat sound the same at the end: -at.
dog and log sound the same at the end: -og.
sun and bun sound the same at the end: -un.
bee and tree sound the same at the end: -ee.
star and car sound the same at the end: -ar.
frog and dog sound the same at the end: -og.

chapters.title: Magic Sight Words
chapters.concept: Some little words pop up everywhere when we read, like the, and, is, you and see. We learn them by heart so reading is super fast!

problems.why: I see a cat. The word see is a sight word.
The dog is big. The word the is a sight word.
I am happy. The word am is a sight word.
It is a ball. The word is is a sight word.
You and I play. The word and joins us together.
We are friends. The word are is a sight word.

chapters.title: Reading Fun
chapters.concept: Let's read little stories and find the answers hidden inside! Tap any word you do not know to hear it. Read like a champion!

problems.q: Read: The cat sat on a mat. The cat is happy. Where did the cat sit?
problems.why: The story says: The cat sat on a mat.

problems.q: Read: Sam has a red ball. He likes to play. What colour is the ball?
problems.why: The story says the ball is red.

problems.q: Read: The sun is hot. We play in the park. Where do we play?
problems.why: The story says: We play in the park.

problems.q: Read: A frog can jump. A fish can swim. What can the frog do?
problems.why: The story says: A frog can jump.

problems.q: Read: Mom made a cake. It is sweet. How does the cake taste?
problems.why: The story says the cake is sweet.

problems.q: Read: Tom has two dogs. They run fast. How many dogs does Tom have?
problems.why: The story says Tom has two dogs.

chapters.title: A and An
chapters.concept: Tiny words with a big job! Use a before most words, like a gift. Use an before words that start with a vowel sound (a, e, i, o, u), like an apple. Easy peasy!

problems.why: Apple starts with the vowel a, so we say an apple.
Egg starts with the vowel e, so we say an egg.
Arrow starts with the vowel a, so we say an arrow.
Gift starts with g, not a vowel sound, so we say a gift.
Ball starts with b, not a vowel sound, so we say a ball.
Umbrella starts with the vowel u, so we say an umbrella.
Orange starts with the vowel o, so we say an orange.
Dog starts with d, not a vowel sound, so we say a dog.

chapters.title: Naming Words
chapters.concept: A noun is a naming word. It names a person, a place, an animal or a thing! Special names like Riya or Delhi always start with a capital letter.

problems.q: Which one is a naming word?
problems.why: A noun names a person, place, animal or thing. dog is an animal, so it is a naming word.

problems.q: Which one is a naming word?
problems.why: school is a place, so it is a naming word.

problems.q: Find the naming word: The cat sleeps.
problems.why: cat is the name of an animal, so it is the naming word.

problems.q: Which one is a special name (proper noun)?
problems.why: Riya is a person's special name, so it is a proper noun. It starts with a capital letter.

problems.q: Which one is a proper noun?
problems.why: Maggi is a special name of a thing, so it is a proper noun.

problems.q: Proper nouns like Riya and Delhi start with a ___ letter.
problems.why: Proper nouns always start with a capital letter.

chapters.title: One and Many
chapters.concept: One puppy, or many puppies! We usually add s to mean more than one, like dog to dogs. But some words are tricky, like child to children!

problems.why: We add s to make it plural: dog becomes dogs.
We add s: egg becomes eggs.
We add s: pencil becomes pencils.
Some words change completely: child becomes children.
Some words change completely: man becomes men.
Some words change completely: tooth becomes teeth.
A few words stay the same: one sheep, many sheep.

chapters.title: Boy or Girl Words
chapters.concept: Some naming words are for boys (masculine) and some are for girls (feminine). A king is a boy and a queen is a girl. Let's match them!

problems.q: The ___ is a very tall man.
problems.why: A man is male, so we use the boy word king.

problems.q: The red ___ is sitting on her eggs.
problems.why: The word her tells us it is female, so we use hen.

problems.q: The ___ is a very beautiful girl.
problems.why: A girl is female, so we use the girl word princess.
The word her tells us it is female, so we use tigress.
Veena is a girl, so she is the daughter.

problems.q: A ___ is the king of the jungle.
problems.why: King is male, so we use the boy word lion.

chapters.title: This, That, These, Those
chapters.concept: These pointing words are like magic fingers! This is one thing near you. That is one thing far away. These is many near you. Those is many far away.

problems.q: A book near you: ___ is a book.
problems.why: It is one book and it is near, so we use This.

problems.q: A book far away: ___ is a book.
problems.why: It is one book and it is far, so we use That.

problems.q: Many books near you: ___ are books.
problems.why: There are many books and they are near, so we use These.

problems.q: Many books far away: ___ are books.
problems.why: There are many books and they are far, so we use Those.

problems.q: ___ flowers near me are so colourful.
problems.why: Many flowers, and near, so we use These.
Many mountains, and far away, so we use Those.

chapters.title: Opposites
chapters.concept: Opposites are words that mean the very different thing! Big is the opposite of small. Hot is the opposite of cold. Let's flip them!

problems.why: Big and small are opposites.
Happy and sad are opposites.
Day and night are opposites.
Wet and dry are opposites.
Fast and slow are opposites.
Near and far are opposites.

problems.q: The stone is heavy but the feather is ___.
problems.why: Heavy and light are opposites.

problems.q: My table is smooth but the sandpaper is ___.
problems.why: Smooth and rough are opposites.

chapters.title: Where Is It?
chapters.concept: Position words tell us where something is! A cat can be on the box, in the box, under it, or behind it. Let's find the spot!

problems.why: The cat is hiding at the back of the box, so it is behind the box.
The girl is below the table, so she is under the table.
The apple is on top of the box, so it is on the box.
The dog is inside the box, so it is in the box.

problems.q: The boy is ___ the two dogs.
problems.why: The boy is in the middle of the two dogs, so he is between them.
The ball is before the box, so it is in front of the box.

chapters.title: Describing Words
chapters.concept: Describing words make things colourful! A funny clown, a big elephant, sweet honey. Adjectives tell us how things look, feel, taste and sound!

problems.q: Rolo is a funny clown. Which word describes Rolo?
problems.why: funny describes the clown, so it is the describing word.

problems.q: The black dog jumped. Which word is the describing word?
problems.why: black tells us about the dog, so it is the adjective.
soft describes how the kitten feels, so it is a describing word.
sour describes the taste of a lemon.
big describes the size of the elephant.
sweet describes the taste of honey.

chapters.title: Action Words
chapters.concept: Action words are doing words! Run, jump, swim, eat. They tell us what someone is doing. Words for yesterday are in the past tense. Let's get moving!

problems.q: Find the action word: The boy runs.
problems.why: runs tells what the boy is doing, so it is the action word.
playing is a doing word, so it is the verb.

problems.q: A verb is also called a ___ word.
problems.why: A verb is a doing word or action word.
For yesterday we use the past tense: play becomes played.
Some verbs change in the past: run becomes ran.

problems.q: Which one is an action word?
problems.why: jump is a doing word, so it is the verb.

chapters.title: Joining Words
chapters.concept: Joining words link words and sentences together! Use and to join, like bread and butter. Use but to show a difference, and because to give a reason!

problems.why: We use and to join two words: lock and key.

problems.q: I have a pear ___ an orange.
problems.why: We use and to join the two things together.

problems.q: I am carrying an umbrella ___ it is raining.
problems.why: because shows the reason why she is carrying an umbrella.

problems.q: I like tea ___ I do not like coffee.
problems.why: but shows a difference between the two parts.

problems.q: We use ___ to join two words.
problems.why: and is the joining word.

problems.q: We use ___ to show a reason.
problems.why: because tells us the reason for something.

chapters.title: Fun Similes
chapters.concept: A simile compares two things using the word as or like. We say as busy as a bee, or as sweet as honey. Similes make our words fun and colourful!
chapters.examples: 🐝 busy as a bee · 🍯 sweet as honey · 🐢 slow as a tortoise

problems.why: Bees are always working, so we say as busy as a bee.
Honey is very sweet, so we say as sweet as honey.
A tortoise moves very slowly, so we say as slow as a tortoise.
Snow is very white, so we say as white as snow.
Ice is very cold, so we say as cold as ice.
A fox is very clever, so we say as clever as a fox.
A feather is very light, so we say as light as a feather.
A kitten is very soft, so we say as soft as a kitten.

chapters.title: Capital Letters and Full Stops
chapters.concept: Every sentence starts with a CAPITAL letter and ends with a full stop. Names of people and places start with a capital letter too. Let's write it right!
chapters.examples: Riya is happy. · I have a dog.

problems.why: It starts with a capital T and ends with a full stop.

problems.q: A sentence always ends with a ___.
problems.why: We put a full stop at the end of every sentence.

problems.q: A sentence always begins with a ___ letter.
problems.why: The first letter of a sentence is always a capital letter.
Names of people start with a capital letter.
The word I is always written as a capital letter.
Capital letter at the start, full stop at the end.

chapters.title: Asking Words
chapters.concept: Question words help us ask things! Who asks about a person, what asks about a thing, where asks about a place, and when asks about time.

problems.why: We ask What is your name to know the name.
Who asks about a person.
Where asks about a place.
When asks about time.
What asks about a thing.
Who asks about a person.

chapters.title: How Much, How Many
chapters.concept: Some words tell us how much or how many. We use many for things we can count, like apples. We use much for things we cannot count, like water.

problems.why: Friends can be counted, so we use many.

problems.q: There is ___ water in the glass.
problems.why: Water cannot be counted, so we use little, not many.
Books can be counted, so we use many.

problems.q: There is not ___ rice in the bowl.
problems.why: Rice cannot be counted one by one, so we use much.
Some tells us an amount of things we can count.
a lot of means a big number of things.

chapters.title: Order Words
chapters.concept: Order words tell us what happens first and what comes next! We say First, Next, Then and Finally to put things in the right order.

problems.q: Which word means it happens at the very start?
problems.why: First tells us what happens at the very beginning.

problems.q: Which word means it happens at the very end?
problems.why: Finally tells us what happens at the very end.

problems.q: First, squeeze the lemon. ___, add sugar.
problems.why: Next tells us what comes after the first step.

problems.q: First I wake up. ___ I brush my teeth.
problems.why: Then tells us the next step.

problems.q: First I put on socks. ___ I put on shoes.
problems.why: Then tells us what comes next.

problems.q: The order words are First, Next, Then and ___.
problems.why: The four order words are First, Next, Then, Finally.

chapters.title: Colours All Around
chapters.concept: Colours are everywhere! The sky is blue, grass is green and the sun is yellow. Let's learn our colour words!

problems.why: The sky is blue.
Grass is green.
The sun looks yellow.
A ripe banana is yellow.
This apple is red.
Snow is white.

chapters.title: Days of the Week
chapters.concept: There are seven days in a week: Sunday, Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday and Saturday. Let's learn them in order!
chapters.examples: Mon · Tue · Wed · Thu · Fri · Sat · Sun

problems.q: How many days are there in a week?
problems.why: There are seven days in a week.
After Monday comes Tuesday.
After Sunday comes Monday.
Before Friday comes Thursday.

problems.q: Which one is a day of the week?
problems.why: Wednesday is a day of the week.
Saturday and Sunday are the weekend.

chapters.title: Baby Animals
chapters.concept: Baby animals have special names! A baby dog is a puppy, a baby cat is a kitten and a baby cow is a calf. Let's meet the babies!

problems.q: A baby dog is called a ___?
problems.why: A baby dog is a puppy.

problems.q: A baby cat is called a ___?
problems.why: A baby cat is a kitten.

problems.q: A baby cow is called a ___?
problems.why: A baby cow is a calf.

problems.q: A baby hen is called a ___?
problems.why: A baby hen is a chick.

problems.q: A baby frog is called a ___?
problems.why: A baby frog is a tadpole.

problems.q: A baby lion is called a ___?
problems.why: A baby lion is a cub.

chapters.title: Animal Sounds
chapters.concept: Animals make different sounds! A dog says woof, a cat says meow and a cow says moo. Let's listen to the animals!

problems.q: What sound does a dog make?
problems.why: A dog says woof.

problems.q: What sound does a cat make?
problems.why: A cat says meow.

problems.q: What sound does a cow make?
problems.why: A cow says moo.

problems.q: What sound does a duck make?
problems.why: A duck says quack.

problems.q: What sound does a sheep make?
problems.why: A sheep says baa.

problems.q: What sound does a lion make?
problems.why: A lion roars.

chapters.title: Fruits and Vegetables
chapters.concept: We eat yummy fruits and vegetables to stay strong! An apple and a banana are fruits. A carrot and a potato are vegetables. Let's sort them!

problems.q: Is an apple a fruit or a vegetable?
problems.why: An apple grows on a tree and is a fruit.

problems.q: Is a carrot a fruit or a vegetable?
problems.why: A carrot grows under the ground and is a vegetable.
A banana is a fruit.
A tomato is a vegetable.

problems.q: Which fruit is yellow and long?
problems.why: A banana is yellow and long.

problems.q: We eat fruits and vegetables to stay ___.
problems.why: Fruits and vegetables keep us healthy and strong.

chapters.title: My Body
chapters.concept: Our body has many parts! We see with our eyes, hear with our ears and smell with our nose. Let's name the parts!

problems.why: We see with our eyes.
We hear with our ears.
We smell with our nose.
We walk with our feet.
We taste with our tongue.
We clap with our hands.

chapters.title: Animal Homes
chapters.concept: Every animal has a home! A bird lives in a nest, a bee lives in a hive and a lion lives in a den. Let's find their homes!

problems.why: A bird lives in a nest.
A bee lives in a hive.
A spider lives in a web.
A lion lives in a den.
A horse lives in a stable.
A dog lives in a kennel.

chapters.title: Facts and Opinions
chapters.concept: A fact is something that is always true and can be checked. An opinion is what someone feels or likes. Let's tell them apart!
chapters.examples: Fact: The sun is hot. · Opinion: Ice cream is the best!

problems.why: The sun being hot is always true, so it is a fact.

options: I think dogs are the best!
problems.a: I think dogs are the best!
problems.why: It is what someone feels, so it is an opinion.

problems.q: Water is wet. Is this a fact or an opinion?
problems.why: Water is always wet, so it is a fact.

problems.q: Pizza is the tastiest food. Is this a fact or an opinion?
problems.why: Tastiest is what someone likes, so it is an opinion.

problems.q: A week has seven days. Is this a fact or an opinion?
problems.why: A week always has seven days, so it is a fact.

problems.q: Blue is the prettiest colour. Is this a fact or an opinion?
problems.why: Prettiest is a feeling, so it is an opinion.
